meta.title: Unit 1 — Singular & Plural Nouns (Test 5)
meta.subject: English Grammar
meta.description: Mix of plural rules: choose the correct form in context sentences.

questions.question: What is the plural of "book"?
questions.explanation: book → books. Regular nouns add -s.

questions.question: "Glasses" is the plural of "glass".
questions.explanation: Yes! glass ends in -ss, so we add -es: glass → glasses.

questions.question: Write the plural of "apple".
questions.explanation: apple → apples. Regular nouns add -s.

questions.question: Mom buys ___ apple. (one apple)
questions.explanation: "one apple" needs the article "an" before a singular vowel-sound noun.

questions.question: "Cats" is a plural noun.
questions.explanation: Yes! "cats" is the plural of "cat" — more than one cat.

questions.question: Write the plural of "wish".
questions.explanation: wish → wishes. Words ending in -sh add -es.

questions.question: Luke and Ella ride their ___ home. (bike)
questions.explanation: Two people means they each have a bike: bikes (plural).

questions.question: Sort these words: Singular or Plural?
questions.explanation: apple/book/boy = one (singular). wishes/glasses = more than one (plural).

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct sentence: "She finds ten shells on the beach."

questions.question: The plural of "road" is "roades".
questions.explanation: road → roads. Regular nouns just add -s; no -es needed here.

questions.question: Write the plural of "ant".
questions.explanation: ant → ants. Regular nouns add -s.

questions.question: Which noun does NOT add -es to form its plural?
questions.explanation: tree → trees (just add -s). The others end in -x, -ch, -s and need -es.

questions.question: Sort these plurals: did they add -s or -es?
questions.explanation: Regular nouns add -s. Nouns ending in -sh or -ss add -es.

questions.question: In the sentence "I open my book to page 8," the noun "book" is singular.
questions.explanation: "my book" — just one book. That's singular.

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct sentence: "Mom buys seven apples."

questions.question: Write the plural of "ruler".
questions.explanation: ruler → rulers. Regular nouns add -s.

questions.question: Which sentence is correct?
options: Roy has two glass of juice.
Roy has two glasses of juice.
Roy has two glasss of juice.
Roy has two glassies of juice.
questions.explanation: glass → glasses (add -es). "two" tells us we need a plural.

questions.question: Which word ends in -sh and adds -es to become plural?
questions.explanation: brush ends in -sh, so brush → brushes (add -es).

questions.question: In the sentence "She has one box," the noun "box" is plural.
questions.explanation: "one box" means just one box — that's singular.

questions.question: Write the plural of "watch".
questions.explanation: watch → watches. Words ending in -ch add -es.
